title: Caucalis bifrons
taxonomy: Caucalis bifrons is classified under the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and similarities to other plants within the same family. The genus Caucalis is a small group of plants that are native to Europe and Asia, and Caucalis bifrons is one of the species within this genus. Caucalis bifrons is further classified as a biennial herb, meaning it completes its life cycle in two years. As a member of the Apiaceae family, Caucalis bifrons shares characteristics such as compound leaves and umbrella-like flower clusters.
uses: Caucalis bifrons, also known as two-flowered horned poppy, has been used in traditional medicine. However, its medicinal uses are largely anecdotal and have not been extensively studied. intro: Caucalis bifrons, also known as wild carrot, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Apiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress.
